source_systems.description: Enterprise Resource Planning system. The source of truth for what products exist, how many are in stock, and what is being manufactured. Based on the AdventureWorks dataset representing a fictional bicycle manufacturer.

tables.name: products
tables.description: Product master catalog. Every item the company manufactures, sells, or stocks has one row here. This is the central table that connects all three source systems through product_id.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the product (e.g. '707'). Primary key. Referenced by erp.inventory_levels, erp.work_orders, procurement.po_lines, and wms.stock_movements.

columns.name: product_name
columns.description: Full descriptive name of the product (e.g. 'Sport-100 Helmet, Red')

columns.name: sku
columns.description: Stock Keeping Unit code — short alphanumeric code used in day-to-day operations (e.g. 'HL-U509-R')

columns.name: unit_cost
columns.description: Cost to manufacture this product, in US dollars

columns.name: list_price
columns.description: Price charged to customers, in US dollars

columns.name: safety_stock
columns.description: Minimum quantity that must be on hand before triggering a restock alert

columns.name: reorder_point
columns.description: Stock level at which a new production or purchase order should be created

columns.name: product_line
columns.description: Product family code: R=Road bikes, M=Mountain bikes, T=Touring bikes, S=Standard/other

columns.name: product_class
columns.description: Quality tier: H=High, M=Medium, L=Low

columns.name: size
columns.description: Physical size (e.g. '58', 'L', 'XL'). Blank if not applicable.

columns.name: weight_lbs
columns.description: Weight in pounds. Blank if not applicable.

tables.name: inventory_levels
tables.description: Current stock on hand. Shows how many units of each product are at each specific shelf and bin within a warehouse location. One row per unique product + location + shelf + bin combination.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.products.product_id. Identifies which product is stocked here.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.locations.location_id. Identifies where this stock is held.

columns.name: shelf
columns.description: Shelf label within the location (e.g. 'A', 'B')

columns.name: bin
columns.description: Bin number within the shelf — a specific slot or container (e.g. '1', '2')

columns.name: qty_on_hand
columns.description: Number of units currently in this location

columns.name: last_updated
columns.description: Date this stock count was last recorded or updated

tables.name: locations
tables.description: Warehouse and production floor locations where inventory can be held. Referenced by both erp.inventory_levels and wms.stock_movements.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the location. Primary key. Referenced by erp.inventory_levels and wms.stock_movements.

columns.name: location_name
columns.description: Human-readable name (e.g. 'Tool Crib', 'Finished Goods Storage', 'Frame Forming')

columns.name: cost_rate
columns.description: Hourly operating cost for this location, in US dollars per hour

columns.name: capacity
columns.description: Available working capacity in hours per week

tables.name: work_orders
tables.description: Production work orders. Each row is an instruction to the factory floor to manufacture a given quantity of a product by a deadline. Tracks the full lifecycle from start to completion.

columns.name: wo_id
columns.description: Unique work order identifier. Primary key.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.products.product_id. Identifies which product is being manufactured.

columns.name: qty_required
columns.description: Number of units ordered to be produced

columns.name: qty_stocked
columns.description: Number of units completed and moved into stock

columns.name: qty_scrapped
columns.description: Number of units rejected or discarded during production

columns.name: start_date
columns.description: Date production actually began (or was planned to begin)

columns.name: end_date
columns.description: Date production actually finished

columns.name: due_date
columns.description: Deadline by which the order must be completed

source_systems.description: Procurement system. Manages purchasing goods from external suppliers — the approved vendor list, purchase orders, and the individual line items on each order. Based on the AdventureWorks dataset.

tables.name: vendors
tables.description: Approved supplier list. Only vendors on this list are authorized to receive purchase orders from the company.

columns.name: vendor_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the vendor. Primary key. Referenced by procurement.purchase_orders.

columns.name: account_number
columns.description: The company's internal account number assigned to this vendor

columns.name: vendor_name
columns.description: The vendor's company name

columns.name: credit_rating
columns.description: Credit rating from 1 to 5: 1=Superior, 2=Excellent, 3=Above Average, 4=Average, 5=Below Average

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: True if the vendor is currently authorized to receive purchase orders

columns.name: is_preferred
columns.description: True if the vendor has preferred status (better terms, priority ordering)

columns.name: last_updated
columns.description: Date the vendor record was last modified

tables.name: purchase_orders
tables.description: Purchase order headers. Each row represents one PO — the top-level agreement to buy goods from a vendor. Line-item details are in procurement.po_lines.

columns.name: po_id
columns.description: Unique Purchase Order identifier. Primary key. Referenced by procurement.po_lines.

columns.name: vendor_id
columns.description: FK — links to procurement.vendors.vendor_id. Identifies which vendor this PO was sent to.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current state as text: PENDING (created), APPROVED (authorized), REJECTED (denied), COMPLETE (fulfilled)

columns.name: status_code
columns.description: Numeric version of status: 1=PENDING, 2=APPROVED, 3=REJECTED, 4=COMPLETE

columns.name: order_date
columns.description: Date the PO was created

columns.name: ship_date
columns.description: Date the goods were shipped by the vendor

columns.name: subtotal
columns.description: Order value before tax, in US dollars

columns.name: tax_amount
columns.description: Tax charged on this order, in US dollars

columns.name: total_due
columns.description: Total amount owed to the vendor including tax, in US dollars

tables.name: po_lines
tables.description: Purchase order line items. Each PO can cover multiple products. One row per product per PO. Records what was ordered, at what price, and how much was received, rejected, and accepted into stock.

columns.name: po_id
columns.description: FK — links to procurement.purchase_orders.po_id. Identifies which PO this line belongs to.

columns.name: line_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this individual line within the PO. Primary key.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.products.product_id. Identifies which product was ordered. Cross-schema FK.

columns.name: qty_ordered
columns.description: Number of units ordered on this line

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: Agreed price per unit for this order, in US dollars

columns.name: qty_received
columns.description: Number of units physically received from the vendor

columns.name: qty_rejected
columns.description: Number of received units that failed inspection and were rejected

columns.name: qty_stocked
columns.description: Number of units that passed inspection and were accepted into stock

columns.name: due_date
columns.description: Date by which delivery was expected

source_systems.description: Warehouse Management System. Tracks the physical movement of stock in and out of warehouses — receipts, shipments, and manual adjustments. This schema is fully synthetic (generated data, not from AdventureWorks).

tables.name: warehouses
tables.description: Physical warehouse facilities the company operates. Reference table for high-level location context (distinct from erp.locations which tracks production floor locations).

columns.name: warehouse_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the warehouse (e.g. 'WH-EAST'). Primary key.

columns.name: warehouse_name
columns.description: Descriptive name (e.g. 'East Distribution Center')

columns.name: city
columns.description: City and state where the warehouse is located

columns.name: capacity_sqft
columns.description: Total floor area of the warehouse in square feet

tables.name: stock_movements
tables.description: Log of every inventory movement event — goods received from a vendor, shipped to a customer, or adjusted due to a count correction. One row per movement event. Generated using product and location combinations from erp.inventory_levels.

columns.name: movement_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this movement event. Primary key.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.products.product_id. Identifies which product moved. Cross-schema FK.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: FK — links to erp.locations.location_id. Identifies which warehouse location the product moved to or from. Cross-schema FK.

columns.name: movement_type
columns.description: Kind of movement: RECEIPT (goods arrived from a vendor), SHIPMENT (goods sent out to a customer), ADJUSTMENT (manual correction to stock count)

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: Number of units involved in this movement

columns.name: movement_date
columns.description: Date the movement occurred

columns.name: reference_doc
columns.description: Document that authorized or triggered this movement (e.g. a PO number or shipment ID)

notes.central_table: erp.products is the central table — it connects all three schemas through product_id
notes.location_note: erp.locations covers production floor locations. wms.warehouses covers distribution center facilities. Both are referenced by wms.stock_movements via location_id.
notes.data_origin: erp and procurement schemas use AdventureWorks data with renamed columns. wms schema is fully synthetic.
